ddls:
CREATE TABLE "geographic_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geographic level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographic level (e.g., entity, district, municipality, section) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the geographic level */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the geographic level is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the geographic level was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the geographic level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "entities" (
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each entity */,
  "entity_name" TEXT /* Name of the entity */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographic level (always 1 for entities) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the entity */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the entity in square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the entity's central point */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the entity's central point */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the entity is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the entity was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the entity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_entities_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES geographic_levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the entity that the district belongs to */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographic level (always 2 for districts) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the district */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the district in square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the district's central point */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the district's central point */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the district is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the district was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the district */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_districts_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES geographic_levels ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_districts_entity_id FOREIGN KEY ("entity_id") REFERENCES entities ("entity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "municipalities" (
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each municipality */,
  "municipality_name" TEXT /* Name of the municipality */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district that the municipality belongs to */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographic level (always 3 for municipalities) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the municipality */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the municipality in square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the municipality's central point */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the municipality's central point */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the municipality is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the municipality was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the municipality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("municipality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_municipalities_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_municipalities_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES geographic_levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sections" (
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each section */,
  "section_name" TEXT /* Name of the section */,
  "municipality_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the municipality that the section belongs to */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the geographic level (always 4 for sections) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the section */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the section in square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the section's central point */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the section's central point */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the section is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the section was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the section */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("section_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sections_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES geographic_levels ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sections_municipality_id FOREIGN KEY ("municipality_id") REFERENCES municipalities ("municipality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic data entry */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the section that the data belongs to */,
  "clavegeo" TEXT /* Clavegeo (unique identifier) for the geographic location */,
  "total_population" INTEGER /* Total population of the section */,
  "male_population" INTEGER /* Male population of the section */,
  "female_population" INTEGER /* Female population of the section */,
  "age_distribution" TEXT /* JSON object storing the age distribution of the population */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* JSON object storing the education level distribution of the population */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the demographic data is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the demographic data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the demographic data */,
  "potras_rel" INTEGER /* Number of people with a certain religion */,
  "pcatolica" INTEGER /* Number of Catholics in the section */,
  "psin_relig" INTEGER /* Number of people without a religion */,
  "pncatolica" INTEGER /* Number of non-Catholics in the section */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_data_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "religions" (
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each religion */,
  "religion_name" TEXT /* Name of the religion */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the religion */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the religion is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the religion was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the religion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("religion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access log is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access log was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the access log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES demographic_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "spatial_data" (
  "spatial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each spatial data entry */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the section that the spatial data belongs to */,
  "geometry" TEXT /* Spatial geometry data (e.g., polygon, point) for the section */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the spatial data */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the spatial data */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the spatial data is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the spatial data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the spatial data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spatial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_spatial_data_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "spatial_references" (
  "reference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each spatial reference */,
  "spatial_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the spatial data entry that the reference belongs to */,
  "reference_name" TEXT /* Name of the spatial reference (e.g., satellite image, map) */,
  "reference_url" TEXT /* URL of the spatial reference */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the spatial reference */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the spatial reference */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the spatial reference is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the spatial reference was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the spatial reference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_spatial_references_spatial_id FOREIGN KEY ("spatial_id") REFERENCES spatial_data ("spatial_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boundary_data" (
  "boundary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each boundary data entry */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the section that the boundary data belongs to */,
  "boundary_type" TEXT /* Type of boundary data (e.g., administrative, physical) */,
  "boundary_value" TEXT /* Value of the boundary data (e.g., city limit, river) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the boundary data */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the boundary data */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the boundary data is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the boundary data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the boundary data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("boundary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_boundary_data_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id")
)
